think she's still working on it.

So any questions there at all?
Anything I can answer before moving on?
So e-book deliverable on Friday.
What should we...
What's the format on that?
What's the, I guess, the desired process for...
For that project, you know, obviously it's, you know, the first, you know, stab you guys are taking at one of these.
So I just want to make sure that we're aligned on expectations for that.
Yeah, sure.
So we actually did an e-book back in February, I think, based on the webinar with Soham.
So the way we did it back then was we just delivered the text itself.
So we had the TOC upfront and then, you know, the clickable TOC, as you mentioned in the outline, and then just basically the text for all the sections.
We also this time have included some image suggestions, which you can either sort of turn into your own assets or other suggestions, which include like product images, et cetera.
And then, yeah, it's just, it's kind of like reviewing an article, of course, but it's a bit more, you know, more words involved.
And yeah, that's, that's kind of how it goes.
Cool.
Sounds great.
All right.
So moving on to the Luca report, which is here.
So, yeah, for this week, we saw pretty much the same number of clicks as last week, give or take a few.
Impressions have been rising quite steadily, and I'll address this shortly.
But generally speaking, we're kind of happy with this performance.
Obviously, clicks are not there, but the steep rise in impressions is sort of a positive for us, just given the way that AI has impacted search.
And just to sort of preview what I'm going to show shortly, we are showing up quite a bit in AI overviews as well, along with this is over and above the LLM referral traffic that we've been getting.
So that really has been creating this spike in impressions, which is this turquoise line over here.
And this is.

I think across a lot of accounts, we're seeing generally, you know, the gap between impressions and clicks won pretty significantly because Google has expanded AI overviews to far more keywords and searches over this past month.
So that gap has actually been increasing quite a bit and we're keeping an eye on it.
And that's one of the reasons why I sort of follow up with Evan regarding like new ways of reporting.
Yeah.
Because it just makes sense now to track conversions as opposed to sticking to just clicks.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I I reached out to Ashley.
I haven't heard back from her yet, but I know that we need to figure out some of that some of that process with with Evan here.
Yeah.

I think he's just super busy.
So he mentioned that the Qualified Dashboard was a quarterly thing and
The way he phrased it, I think he was trying to say that it doesn't make sense to track it on a weekly basis because it gets built or updated or something, I think, quarterly.
I may have understood this wrong.
So I'm still following up with him regarding GA4 events because all of our blogs kind of lead to that.
Also think of adding a few other metrics from GA4 in here.

For example, tracking the rise in branded spaces is a pretty good data point because just given that the impressions are increasing so much, we can't tie the rise in branded searches exactly to our content, but it should have an impact.
Usually a good SEO campaign sort of impacts awareness.
The other thing to check is maybe the number of returning users who are directly typing prophecy's URL into their browsers.
So GA4 actually has this.
I just need to dig through it and find that data.
But between these data points and hopefully the ebook downloads, we can form a good picture of exactly what's going on.
So the GA4 data also kind of says pretty much the same thing.
Over the past four weeks, as we can see here, there definitely has been a bump in traffic.
And again, the number of sessions, it's sort of increasing as well.
So that's a good thing overall.
The number of engaged sessions is also a good sign.
This number from Bing over here could obviously be better, but the number of overall sessions are also quite low compared to Google.
Generally speaking, as I mentioned, 50% is a good benchmark.
We're kind of at the benchmark right now, which is a good thing.
So generally the data sort of is saying a lot of positive things.
In addition, I dug in a little bit and because just to sort of track my thought process here, the rise in impressions and the gap widening between clicks, initially I was a bit alarmed.
So then what I did is we dug in a little bit.
So I'm like, okay, is this having an...
In is this a result, sorry, of AI Overviews?
It does seem to be the case because I've included these sort of articles over here, these URLs.
These articles are showing up in AI Overviews across a number of keywords.
So it's not just one or two.
And the combined volume of all those keywords is in the second column here.
So generally speaking, for a total keyword cluster that has about 41,000 searches a month, we are showing up in AI Overviews.
And it's pretty much the stuff that we want to be showing up for.
obviously the data engineering skills article is showing up everywhere at this point.
And then we have the batch and stream processing, the data pipeline modernization, you know, ELT and ETL articles, self-service and, you know, ETL modernization.
So generally speaking, it seems like we're showing up for all the good things.
And based on the data we have right now, it seems like we are having a positive impact.
But the...
The challenge, of course, is we can't tie it on a one-to-one basis, but that's kind of always been the case with SEO and the data that we get from Google.
So, yes, lots of information.
Happy to answer any questions you may have.
Okay, so this is good.
Can you remind me, GSC versus GA4?
We're looking at both of these because why?
So the GSC gives us only Google traffic, so it gives us only hard performance on Google search.
GA4 is a more well-rounded thing.
tracks traffic from LLMs, from Bing, from other search engines, etc.
So that's the main difference.

If you're telling me GA4 has everything, including Google, can we just look at that, or no, it's not possible?
GA4 doesn't have everything.
It actually has different metrics.
So GA4 kind of metrics what's happening on your site.
GSC measures what happened just before people came onto your site.
So, for example, keywords that people searched, GA4 doesn't give us that.
So that's why we're kind of looking at both of them.
All right.
So that's the performance update.
